rationale: The cited paper is not merely adjacent background: it develops a mechanism for stimulus-to-product transfer (“product contagion” via perceived contact), while the citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending prior spillover research on product evaluations. Both notes focus on how associated cues alter consumer evaluations of target products, so this is plausibly a theory/mechanism source rather than an incidental mention. The exact DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.44.2.272) confirms this specific study was intentionally referenced.
